behavior_in_task_description.reason: All tested specifics (function name Update-VerificationLabels, label constants $LabelConfirmed/$LabelFailed, label values ai-reproduction-confirmed/ai-reproduction-failed, $foundPR variable, '## PR Labels' section, file paths) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required behaviors and API choices (DELETE/POST, $LASTEXITCODE) but doesn't show the implementation code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact variable names like $labelToAdd, $labelToRemove, $foundPR, and exact label string values 'ai-reproduction-confirmed'/'ai-reproduction-failed' — though instruction does specify these, the test also requires literal 'DELETE' and 'POST' strings in function body which constrains implementation; however these are all spelled out in instruction so alternatives that follow instruction would pass. Borderline pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All f2p tests are static text/regex inspections of PS1 and SKILL.md content (e.g., 'DELETE' not in body, '$foundPR' not in content) — no execution of PowerShell or behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require multiple specific structural elements (function with toggle vars, REST API verbs, label constants, calls on multiple paths, doc section) that a stub cannot trivially satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each instruction requirement (function logic, constants, 4 calls, PR fallback, SKILL.md section) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under labeled comment sections (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) with descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies scripts and markdown, not structured data outputs.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers in instruction match those tested.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify (which is appropriate scoping), not specific lines.
